excerpt: No historical record exists of anyone dismissing the Qur'an as dull or poorly expressed. Why? Because of its style, its incomparable manner of speech.
source_location_hint: On the rhetorical inimitability of the Qur'an

excerpt: The true beauty and majesty of Allah's words can never be fully translated. Something of the meaning may cross into another tongue, but the actual force—the true impact that the original possesses—no translation in any language can capture.
source_location_hint: On the untranslatability of the Qur'anic text and its stylistic excellence

excerpt: What was it that mesmerized those people? What caused them, each time the Messenger opened his mouth, to fall into stunned silence, unable to speak?
source_location_hint: On the Qur'an's power over the Arabs of the period

excerpt: The account of 'Utbi ibn Rabi'ah's encounter with the Prophet and his testimony to the Qur'an's extraordinary power to transform even the heart of its opponents.
source_location_hint: On the historical attestation of the Qur'an's miraculous eloquence

excerpt: 'The eyes perceive Him not, nor can imagination grasp Him'—yet despite such distance, His nearness is absolute.
source_location_hint: On the transcendence and immanence of the Divine

excerpt: Your Lord is Allah, who created the heavens and the earth in six days, then established Himself upon the Throne. He covers the night with the day, which follows it swiftly.
source_location_hint: Sermons of Imam ʿAlī on divine creation and lordship

excerpt: He chose Adam from all the universe. He chose the Arabs from among Adam's descendants. He chose Mudar from among the Arabs. He chose Quraysh from among Mudar. He chose Banu Hashim from among Quraysh. And then from Banu Hashim, He chose Muhammad.
source_location_hint: On the divine election of the Prophet and his family

excerpt: When a man dies, his final thoughts turn to his children. When he expires, he remembers his wealth. At the moment of death, each recalls his beloved. But when our Prophet was dying—whom did he remember? His people.
source_location_hint: On the Prophet's compassion for his community at the hour of death

excerpt: He asked Allah: 'What will become of my Ummah?' Allah answered: 'We shall not leave your Ummah alone.'
source_location_hint: On Allah's protection of the Prophet's community

excerpt: God propoundeth a similitude: a town which was secure and tranquil, receiving its provision in abundance from every quarter—yet it turned ungrateful for Allah's favours, so Allah made it taste hunger and fear because of what they wrought.
source_location_hint: Chapter 16 (Sūrat al-Naḥl), verse 112

excerpt: The path to receiving Allah's support, strength, and aid springs not from without but from within the heart. One must populate the inner world. Do the prophets not lead the two worlds, yet the Prophet concealed himself on the journey from Mecca to Medina.
source_work: General Qur'ānic and Prophetic teaching on tawakkul (reliance on Allah)
source_location_hint: Qur'ān and Prophetic tradition on divine trust

excerpt: When Abraham was cast into the furnace, Gabriel came. Allah did not extinguish the fire; rather, He extracted the very effect of burning from the blazing flames, so that the fire's chemical nature was transformed.
source_location_hint: Chapter 21 (Sūrat al-Anbiyāʾ), verses 68–69

excerpt: When Allah says, 'O you who believe' (Yā ayyuhā al-ladhīna āmanū), He wishes to convey something to you and tell you what to do and what to leave.
source_location_hint: Multiple occurrences throughout the Qur'ān (89 times)

excerpt: I am a human being not free from sin; my tongue may err whether through forgetfulness or deliberate fault
source_location_hint: Sunday Sessions discourse, 26 July 2014

excerpt: The postman metaphor: one accepts a correctly addressed letter without scrutinizing the messenger's moral character; the letter's validity does not depend on the deliverer's failings
source_work: Classical Islamic ethics and parable tradition
source_location_hint: Common exemplum in Islamic pedagogy

excerpt: Do not regard who has spoken; regard what has been spoken. Do not regard who has succeeded; regard how he has succeeded.
source_work: Attributed to Maulana ʿAlī / Nahj al-Balāghah tradition
source_location_hint: ʿAlawī tradition, cited in Islamic discourse

excerpt: Sufficient is Allah as guardian, protector, witness, ally, knower; sufficient is your Lord as guide and helper; is not Allah sufficient for His servant? Allah will suffice them, for He is the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing.
source_location_hint: Qurʾānic attestation across multiple sūras

excerpt: O you who believe, either renounce usury, or take notice of war from Allah and His Messenger.
O Abū Sufyān, I have come to you with the honors of this world and the next.
source_work: Ḥadīth tradition / Sīrat al-Nabī (Life of the Prophet)
source_location_hint: Prophetic address to Abū Sufyān at the Conquest of Mecca

excerpt: The sun runs to its appointed time. That is the decree of the Mighty, the All-Knowing.
Allah does not look at your worship and fasting—He looks at what has settled in your heart; I will look into your heart and see what dwells there.
source_location_hint: Qurʾān 49:12 and Prophetic tradition on the value of sincere intention

excerpt: Allah only meets those whose hearts bow down in prostration along with their foreheads.
source_location_hint: Traditional Islamic devotional teaching

excerpt: Voices will be humbled before the Merciful, and you will hear naught but whispers.
He knows the number of raindrops.
source_work: Islamic tradition / Qurʾānic concept of Divine omniscience
source_location_hint: Related to Qurʾān 6:59 and commentarial tradition

excerpt: Do not think that Allah is heedless of what the wrongdoers do; He only delays them until a day when eyes will be wide open.
And who has made you comprehend what is the Deafening Blast?
Your Lord will come, and the angels rank upon rank.
The son of Adam will be brought on the Day of Resurrection and made to stand before Allah, the Exalted, like a goat brought to slaughter.
source_work: Qurʾān, Sūrat al-ʿAlaq 96:15 and Ḥadīth commentarial tradition
source_location_hint: Tafsīr (exegetical) tradition

excerpt: O son of Adam! I gave you sustenance; I granted you dominion; I bestowed on you My blessings. What have you brought for today?
source_location_hint: Dialogical tradition of the Day of Judgment

excerpt: Show Me what you have sent forward; show Me what you have sent forward.
source_work: Qurʾānic and Ḥadīth tradition on deeds sent forward for the Hereafter
source_location_hint: Concept of taqdīm (sending forward) in Qurʾānic parlance

excerpt: My Lord, I gathered much; I earned much; I accumulated much; I left it behind in plenty.
source_work: Ḥadīth and Islamic discourse on worldly accumulation vs. afterlife reward
source_location_hint: Thematic teaching on attachment to this world

excerpt: It will be said: Go back and seek light. But a wall will be erected between them, whereon is a gate, the inner side whereof containeth mercy, while the outer side thereof is toward the doom.
O you who have believed, avoid much of suspicion; indeed, some suspicion is sin. And do not spy, nor backbite one another. Would any of you like to eat the flesh of his brother while dead?
وَمَا ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَآ إِلَّا لَهْوٌ وَلَعِبٌ
source_location_hint: Quranic verse on the nature of worldly life

excerpt: حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا جَآءَهُۥ لَمْ يَجِدْ شَيْئًا
source_location_hint: Quranic description of the mirage metaphor

excerpt: The house of the spider is the weakest of all houses
source_location_hint: Quranic reference to spider's web as symbol of frailty

excerpt: إِنِّىٓ أَنَا۠ رَبُّكَ فَٱخْلَعْ نَعْلَيْكَ ۖ إِنَّكَ بِٱلْوَادِ ٱلْمُقَدَّسِ طُوًۭى۔ وَأَنَا ٱخْتَرْتُكَ فَٱسْتَمِعْ لِمَا يُوحَىٰٓ۔ إِنَّنِىٓ أَنَا ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَهَ إِلَّآ أَنَا۠ فَٱعْبُدْنِى وَأَقِمِ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ لِذِكْرِىٓ۔ إِنَّ ٱلسَّاعَةَ آتِيَةٌ أَكَادُ أُخْفِيهَا لِتُجْزَىٰ كُلُّ نَفْسٍۭ بِمَا تَسْعَىٰ۔ فَلَا يَصُدَّنَّكَ عَنْهَا مَن لَّا يُؤْمِنُ بِهَا وَٱتَّبَعَ هَوَىٰهُ فَتَرْدَىٰ
source_location_hint: Quranic account of Divine address to Moses at Mount Sinai

excerpt: O Lord, expand my breast. Make smooth my tongue that they may understand my speech. Appoint for me a helper from my family—Aaron, my brother...
source_location_hint: Quranic account of Moses's supplication to Allah

excerpt: Peace be upon Noah in the worlds
source_location_hint: Quranic salutation upon Noah

excerpt: Peace be upon Abraham
source_location_hint: Quranic salutation upon Abraham

excerpt: Peace be upon Moses and Aaron
source_location_hint: Quranic salutation upon Moses and Aaron

excerpt: Peace be upon all the Messengers

excerpt: إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ وَمَلَٓئِكَتَهُۥ يُصَلُّونَ عَلَى ٱلنَّبِىِّۚ
source_location_hint: Quranic verse on Divine and angelic blessings upon the Prophet

excerpt: يَٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا۟ صَلُّوا۟ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلِّمُوا۟ تَسْلِيمًا
source_location_hint: Quranic injunction to believers to send blessings upon the Prophet

excerpt: The Lord said: I called to my people day and night
source_location_hint: Quranic account of Noah's constant supplication for his people

excerpt: فَلَمْ يَزِدْهُمْ دُعَآؤُنَآ إِلَّا فِرَارًۭا
source_location_hint: Quranic description of people's rejection of Noah's call

excerpt: جَعَلُوٓا۟ أَصَبِعَهُمْ فِىٓ آذَانِهِمْ وَٱسْتَغْشَوْا۟ ثِيَابَهُمْ وَأَصَرُّوا۟ وَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ ٱسْتِكْبَارًۭا
source_location_hint: Quranic description of people's arrogant rejection of Noah

excerpt: O thou wrapped in garment! Arise and warn!
source_location_hint: Opening of Sura al-Muddathir addressing the Prophet

excerpt: Do not call the Messenger among you as you call one another
source_location_hint: Quranic instruction on addressing the Prophet with respect

excerpt: Your Lord is Allah, the Creator of the heavens and the earth, Who fashioned them for your sustenance. He created mankind from clay.
source_location_hint: Quranic affirmation of Divine creation

excerpt: اللہ الذی خلق سبع سماوات وَرَفَعَ سَمْكَهَا فَسَوَّىٰهَا
source_location_hint: Quranic description of celestial creation

excerpt: أَخْرَجَ مِنْهَا مَآءَهَا وَمَرْعَىٰهَا۔فَأَنۢبَتْنَا فِيهَا حَبًّۭا وَعِنَبًۭا وَقَضْبًۭا وَزَيْتُونًۭا وَنَخْلًۭا وَحَدَآئِقَ غُلْبًۭا وَفَكِهَةًۭ وَأَبًّۭا
source_location_hint: Quranic enumeration of earthly bounties

excerpt: The towel touched by the hands of the Holy Prophet remained unburnt in fire—a sign of the Prophet's sanctity
source_location_hint: Narrative illustrating the Prophet's blessed touch

excerpt: بلا وَ أنَّ جِبرَئِیلَ نَفَسَ فی رُّوحیِ اَنَّ النَّفسَ لَم تموتَ حتّٰی تَستَک مِنَ الرِّزقَھا
source_location_hint: Prophetic teaching on Divine provision

excerpt: My community may commit any transgression, yet they cannot lie and deceive
source_location_hint: Prophetic characterization of the Muslim community

excerpt: The farewell sermon delivered at Ghadir Khumm lasted ten to fifteen minutes before seventy thousand souls
source_location_hint: Historical account of the Prophet's final sermon

excerpt: This is our Qur'an—the Qur'an in human form. If you wish to see the Qur'an made manifest, behold him as he walks before you
source_location_hint: Anecdote illustrating ʿAlī as embodiment of Quranic teaching

excerpt: قُلْ هَذِهِۦ سَبِيلِىٓ أَدْعُوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ ۚ عَلَىٰ بَصِيرَةٍ أَنَا۠ وَمَنِ ٱتَّبَعَنِى ۖ وَسُبْحَنَ ٱللَّهِ وَمَآ أَنَا۠ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ
source_location_hint: Quranic verse on the Prophet's path of invitation to Allah

excerpt: When we first contemplate our own world—this sphere called al-ʿālam al-akbar, the Greater World—we perceive how Allah provides it sustenance.
source_location_hint: Sermons on Divine lordship and cosmic order

excerpt: Had gravity doubled, so that earth's diameter became not twenty-four thousand but forty-eight thousand kilometers, we would walk upon the ground as one walks in soft mud or wet cement.
source_location_hint: Majlis on the wonders of creation and divine lordship

excerpt: We have sent down water in abundance. Then We split the earth in fissures. Then We caused grain to grow in it, and grapes, and herbage, and olives, and palms, and gardens dense with growth.

Allah Almighty dissipates five hundred fifty kilometers per hour of destructive force in the air itself through innumerable resistances, reducing raindrops to eight kilometers per hour at ground level.
source_location_hint: Philosophical reflections on divine order in nature

excerpt: Allah introduced Himself through the attribute of Lordship, commanding: 'Be free from anxiety! Do not grieve! It is My function to sustain you. I distinguish lawful from forbidden, truth from falsehood, honesty from deception, justice from oppression.'
source_location_hint: Teachings on divine providence and human responsibility

excerpt: And when your Lord drew forth from the children of Adam—from their loins—their descendants, and made them testify concerning themselves. Am I not your Lord?
source_location_hint: Quran 7:172

excerpt: Indeed, those who said, 'Our Lord is Allah,' and remained steadfast upon that truth.
source_location_hint: Quran 41:30

excerpt: The angels will descend upon them, saying: 'Do not fear, nor grieve. Rejoice in the Paradise which you were promised.'
source_location_hint: Quran 41:30

excerpt: The One who possesses all these attributes—creator of heavens and earth, sustainer of angels and souls, ruler of life and death—alone deserves to be called Lord, and He is Allah alone.
source_location_hint: Exposition on divine lordship and attributes

excerpt: Say: The impure and the pure are not equal, even though the quantity of impure things may astonish you.
source_location_hint: Quran 5:100

excerpt: The Messenger of Allah observed a merchant concealing wet grain beneath dry grain and commanded: 'Remove it so the buyer knows of the defect.' Then declared: Whoever deceives does not belong to my community.
source_location_hint: Legal traditions on honesty in commerce

excerpt: Truth delivers; lies destroy. Allah's curse is upon the wrongdoers. Allah does not love the deceivers, and Allah does not guide the stratagems of deceivers.
source_location_hint: Supplications on truth and justice

excerpt: Allah does not love the deceivers. Allah does not guide the stratagems of the deceivers.
source_location_hint: Quran 12:52

excerpt: O Allah, Master of Dominion! You grant dominion to whom You will, and You remove dominion from whom You will. You honor whom You will, and You humiliate whom You will. In Your hand is all good. Truly, You have power over all things.
source_location_hint: Quran 3:26

excerpt: You merge the night into the day and the day into the night. You bring the living from the dead, and the dead from the living. And You sustain whom You will without measure.
source_location_hint: Quran 3:27

excerpt: Islam is not a title or label; it is a quality and way of life, requiring transformation of belief and character from the root of the heart.
source_location_hint: Philosophical teachings on the essence of faith

excerpt: The knowledge you have been given is very little. The word autitam (given) indicates that this knowledge has been given to you, and you have not taken it yourself.
source_location_hint: Qur'an, surah al-Isrāʾ

excerpt: Man is very hasty, very prone to dispute, very ungrateful, and very unjust and ignorant. When hardship touches him he despairs, but when ease comes he becomes arrogant.
source_location_hint: Qur'anic verses regarding human nature

excerpt: When We give ease after difficulty, man becomes proud and forgetful of his station, walking upon the earth as if to tear it asunder.
source_location_hint: Qur'an, surah al-Rūm

excerpt: The knowledge of the Prophets comes directly from Allah through revelation, not through study or learning.

excerpt: At Hudaybiyyah, when Ali was writing the treaty, Suhayl ibn Umar objected to 'In the name of Allah, the Most Merciful' and requested it be changed to 'In Your name, O Allah.'

excerpt: Those who came as light, who came as light themselves—from them we must draw our illumination.
